notes: Three agy lifetimes back-to-back, one conv-id per variant (driver: exit_clean, restart+sigkill, restart+mid-turn-sigkill). Antigravity is transcript-first: each variant's conv-id session is born in ready when agy writes the transcript, cycles ready->working->ready over its turn. PID binding is best-effort and NOT asserted, so process_exited / presession proc-<PID> are NOT asserted either: agy reports its sandbox scratch dir (not the launch cwd) as the run_command Cwd, so DiscoverPID never matches the agy process and no PID binds to a conv-id (verified across the 1.1 + 1.2 recordings: zero process_exited despite a clean Ctrl-D and two SIGKILLs). Teardown is observable only as the transcript going quiet plus the eventual readyTTL sweep, which the short tail does not capture; the load-bearing observable for this adapter is the three distinct conv-id session arcs. v3 (SIGKILL mid-turn) is killed while working, so its arc ends in working with no settle-to-ready. Each session_birth is new_session:true so a later variant can't match an earlier ready. Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL); model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> reports 'Gemini 3.1 Pro' on this account.

text: Variant 1: conv-id session appears in ready after the first turn
Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready -> working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready (terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE, no tool_calls); the clean Ctrl-D exit leaves it in ready
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready -> working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready; the SIGKILL mid-idle then leaves it in ready (no process_exited binds for this adapter)
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — user prompt drives ready -> working, then killed mid-turn; the arc ends in working (no settle-to-ready, no process_exited)
